steps.title: Identify what the underlined portion does
steps.content: The passage opens by saying Douglass cautioned technology investors who predict books will be displaced. The underlined portion then states that interactive text is much older than technologists assume—directly pushing back on their assumption. This is a challenge to the investors’ stance, not support for it.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘It challenges the stance of the investors and enthusiasts who are mentioned earlier in the text.’: The underlined portion refutes the investors’ assumption by showing interactive text predates modern technology. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘It explains the basis for the claim made by the technologists mentioned in the text.’: The passage opposes the technologists’ claim rather than supporting it. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘It suggests that academics are better suited than investors to see the potential uses of contemporary interactive texts.’: No comparison between academics and investors appears in the underlined portion. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘It provides a historical ane\cdote about the technological challenges involved in reading the earliest interactive texts.’: The underlined portion is about the age of interactivity, not about technological challenges in reading. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
